context: Subagent in foreign repo (Ruby) used ripgrep for class/constant/method-name searches with regex alternation pattern (Foo|Bar|Baz). Existing iter-5 evals (10/10 PASS) did not catch this regression. Four failure modes identified spanning search-cascade.md AND scripts/enforce-tearags-search.sh (the actual subagent inject hook).

audit_findings.FM1: 'regex' trigger word in ripgrep allowlist causes false positives — agent sees `|` in query and routes to ripgrep regardless of intent. Lives in BOTH search-cascade.md:248 AND enforce-tearags-search.sh:29 — must fix in lockstep.
audit_findings.FM2: Subagent Search Injection block has positive ripgrep cases but no negative prohibitions — no 'ripgrep NEVER for class/method/constant names'. Both files affected.
audit_findings.FM3: Real subagent prompt receives only enforce-tearags-search.sh SUFFIX (~20 lines), NOT search-cascade.md content. SUFFIX lacks intent examples and negative cases — must be self-contained.
audit_findings.FM4: BM25 exact-match capability of hybrid_search (score up to 1.0) not surfaced in either file — agent picks ripgrep when wanting 'exact' match for code identifiers.

out_of_scope: FM5 (Session Start protocol non-firing in Opus 4.7) — already delivered via SessionStart hook (inject-rules.sh). Compliance gap, not text gap. Separate concern.

evals.name: subagent-class-rename-alternation
evals.prompt: [subagent: code-review] Parent renamed class ConvertLinksToAutomoved to Links::ConvertToAutomoved. Find all callers of either old or new name across the codebase.
evals.expected: hybrid_search per name (or one query 'ConvertLinksToAutomoved Links::ConvertToAutomoved'). NOT mcp__ripgrep__search with `ConvertLinksToAutomoved|Links::ConvertToAutomoved` pattern.
evals.failure_mode: Agent sees 2 class names → constructs regex alternation → routes to ripgrep because rule says 'regex → ripgrep'. Misses that intent is 'exhaustive usage of symbol' which is hybrid_search territory.

baseline_no_rules.reason: Picked search_code with regex alternation — no rule to redirect

before_fix_full_cascade.reason: Full cascade has Prohibited Patterns block — over-optimistic

before_fix_suffix_only.reason: Phrasing 'find all callers' triggered Exhaustive usage branch correctly

after_fix_suffix_only.reason: Confirmed via explicit anti-pattern: 'ripgrep with `|` on class names'

evals.name: subagent-constant-multi-name
evals.prompt: [subagent: task-agent] Find all references to RENAMED_MAPPINGS_DIR, RENAMED_MAPPINGS, and renamed_mappings in the codebase. They are constants and a method.
evals.expected: hybrid_search per name (or combined). NOT ripgrep with `RENAMED_MAPPINGS_DIR|RENAMED_MAPPINGS|renamed_mappings` pattern.
evals.failure_mode: Three identifier names → looks like grep job → ripgrep. No explicit prohibition in Subagent Injection block triggers correction.

before_fix_suffix_only.reason: 'all references' phrasing matched Exhaustive usage branch

after_fix_suffix_only.reason: Anti-pattern citation: 'ripgrep on identifier names is anti-pattern'

evals.name: subagent-single-file-symbol
evals.prompt: [subagent: explore] Find all usages of TRIGGER_KINDS constant inside the file app/models/workflow/task.rb only.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'TRIGGER_KINDS', relativePath: 'app/models/workflow/task.rb') OR find_symbol(relativePath: 'app/models/workflow/task.rb') for outline. NOT mcp__ripgrep__search.
evals.failure_mode: Single-file scope + bare constant name → agent thinks 'small scope, exact match → ripgrep'. Subagent block lacks single-file find_symbol guidance.

before_fix_suffix_only.reason: REPRODUCED — picked ripgrep, citing 'Exact text patterns' branch for single-file constant lookup

after_fix_suffix_only.reason: FIXED — Single-file scope is now first matching branch in rule

evals.name: bm25-exact-match-awareness
evals.prompt: Find all callers of class PaymentProcessor in this TypeScript project. I need exact symbol-name matches, not semantic similarity.
evals.expected: hybrid_search query='PaymentProcessor' (rule must surface that BM25 component gives exact-name match score up to 1.0). NOT ripgrep.
evals.failure_mode: User says 'exact match, not semantic' → agent picks ripgrep because hybrid_search description sounds 'semantic'. Rule must explicitly say BM25 inside hybrid_search = exact match.

baseline_no_rules.reason: Picked find_symbol — returns definition, not callers

before_fix_suffix_only.reason: Existing 'BM25 = full recall' phrasing was sufficient

after_fix_suffix_only.reason: Stronger phrasing 'BM25 exact-name match up to 1.0 — strictly better than ripgrep'

evals.name: subagent-class-name-with-pipe-syntax
evals.prompt: [subagent: explore] Find usages of Authenticator|TokenValidator (two adjacent classes that often appear together).
evals.expected: hybrid_search per class name (2 calls or one combined). NOT mcp__ripgrep__search with the literal `Authenticator|TokenValidator` pattern.
evals.failure_mode: User wrote `|` syntax in their query → agent reads it as 'literal regex request' → ripgrep. Must distinguish: query syntax !== intent. Bare class names = symbol search.

baseline_no_rules.reason: Picked ripgrep with `|` literal — natural intuition without rule

before_fix_suffix_only.reason: Agent correctly interpreted `|` as 'two symbols' due to BM25 context

after_fix_suffix_only.reason: Explicit anti-pattern reinforced: 'ripgrep `|` on class names forbidden'

evals.name: control-todo-marker
evals.prompt: Find all TODO and FIXME comments in src/core/.
evals.expected: mcp__ripgrep__search with pattern 'TODO|FIXME' in path src/core/. This IS the legitimate ripgrep use case.
evals.failure_mode: Agent overcorrects after fix and routes literal TODO/FIXME to tea-rags. Must keep ripgrep allowlist intact for actual literal markers.

evals.name: control-import-path-string
evals.prompt: Find all files that have `from "./contracts/errors.js"` import statement.
evals.expected: mcp__ripgrep__search for the literal import string. Legitimate ripgrep — exact path string in code.
evals.failure_mode: Over-restricting ripgrep would break this case. Import path strings stay in ripgrep territory.

evals.name: control-known-symbol-definition
evals.prompt: Show me the implementation of the Reranker class.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'Reranker'). NO Read needed — find_symbol returns full definition. NOT ripgrep, NOT hybrid_search.
evals.failure_mode: Regression check — known symbol stays on find_symbol path.

evals.name: control-behavioral-intent
evals.prompt: Find code that handles retry logic after embedding API failures.
evals.expected: semantic_search query='retry logic embedding failure'. Intent-based, no specific symbol name.
evals.failure_mode: Regression check — pure intent queries stay on semantic_search.

summary.after_fix_suffix_only: 9/9 (100%) — Case 3 fixed via Single-file scope branch
